Beauty queen, man arrested after chase across Europe over $1.7 million wine theft   MADRID — A former Mexican beauty queen and a Romanian-Dutch accomplice have been arrested in Croatia over the theft of $1.7 million worth of prestige bottles of wine in Spain after a nine-month chase across Europe, police said on Wednesday.

Two dead, 10 injured in multi-vehicle crash on highway south of Montreal   BROSSARD, Que. — Quebec provincial police say two people are dead and 10 injured after a multi-vehicle crash Tuesday night on Highway 30, south of Montreal.

Sky-high diesel prices squeeze truckers, farmers, consumers   NEW YORK (AP) — When long-haul trucker Deb LaBree sets out on the road to deliver pharmaceuticals, she has strategies to hold down costs. She avoids the West Coast and the Northeast, where diesel prices are highest. She organizes her delivery route to minimize “deadheading” — driving an empty truck in between deliveries.
Man fatally shot at nightclub hit by stray bullet: Relative   'We need harder sentences for these guys'
Heat warnings stretch into day two for large part of Ontario   A large part of Ontario — including some northern regions — is continuing under a heat warning today.

Canada fall short in Concacaf W Championship final losing to U.S.   Alex Morgan scored the winning goal from the penalty spot in the 78 minute at the BBMV Stadium

2022 WNBA Championship Odds: Vegas Holds Off Chicago For Now   WNBA Championship odds are gaining attention as the 2022 regular season is gearing up for the home stretch, and sportsbooks still see a dogfight among the league’s top teams.

Tiger-Cats vs Lions Week 7 Picks and Predictions: BC Bounces Back Against Turnover-Prone Tabbies   Week 7 in the CFL kicks off with a pair of games on Thursday night, ending with a clash between the Hamilton Tiger-Cats and BC Lions.

Alouettes vs Redblacks Week 7 Picks and Predictions: Als Assert Themselves against Bigger Train Wreck   Week 7 in the CFL kicks off with a pair of games on Thursday night, beginning with a clash between the Montreal Alouettes and Ottawa Redblacks.

2022 MLB Home Run Title Odds: Austin Riley Has Entered the Chat   With the All-Star Game a week away, we’ve officially entered the second half of the MLB calendar. In addition to keeping track of MLB MVP odds , Cy Young odds , MLB Rookie of the Year odds , and MLB playoff odds , we’re also keeping our eyes on the home run totals board.

MLB All-Star Game Picks and Predictions: Hot Pitching Keeps Scoring at Bay   It’s officially the halfway point of the big-league season and that means it’s time for baseball’s best talent to converge at Dodger Stadium in Los Angeles for the annual MLB All-Star Game. And while not everyone is a fan of the Midsummer Classic, it’s basically the only game in town this week. So, you know even casual fans will want to have a wager on it.

How a teen 'vampire's' blog blew up a murder trial — and prevented a wrongful conviction   The blog contained a description of her 'likes,' which included: 'Blood, pain ... rain, darkness, thunder ... fire, my bedroom, sex ... cemeteries, knives'

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.
HAVE YOUR SAY: Answer our daily Toronto Sun poll question
EDITORIAL: Adapting to climate is money well spent   Prime Minister Justin Trudeau visited a cherry farm in B.C this week, where climate change contributed to a smaller crop than usual.

ZWAAGSTRA: It’s time to scrap the ArriveCAN app   Deputy Prime Minister Chrystia Freeland thinks her government is too humble. At least that’s what she said when a reporter asked her why travellers are still forced to fill out the ArriveCAN app before entering Canada.

OPINION: Trudeau and Biden must make dramatic U-turns to mitigate recession   There are increasing signs that Canada, like most western countries, is heading for a recession, if we’re not in one already.

LILLEY: Report says Ontario's health spending is both up and down -- and it's why we need change   If you wanted a clear picture of Ontario’s finances, you’d be hard pressed to get one. Despite reports from the provincial finance department, the legislature’s finance committee and the Financial Accountability Office, most people wouldn’t have a clue about provincial spending.

Linda Evangelista settles lawsuit after claiming cosmetic procedure left her 'deformed'   Linda Evangelista has settled her CoolSculpting lawsuit after claiming the fat-reduction procedure left her “deformed.”
Ethan Hawke says Marvel films are 'actor-friendly,' defends critics   Ethan Hawke insists the Marvel Cinematic Universe is “actor-friendly” rather than appealing to directors.

SUNshine Girl Briar   SUNshine Girl Briar is back for more! This Capricorn enjoys hiking and running. Her dream car is a Civic Si and she’d like to one day be a coroner. Briar posed for us in late February 2020. (Dave Abel/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Kate   SUNshine Girl Kate is a lover of the great outdoors, counting camping and fishing among her fave pastimes. This brown-eyed girl once met country superstars Toby Keith and Miranda Lambert. (Dave Abel/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl Crystal   SUNshine Girl Crystal is back once again! She likes to relax in cozy PJs — that is, when she's not horseback riding or going for a rip on an ATV. She loves hockey and baseball, while her dream car is a Lamborghini and her dream date is Channing Tatum. (Dave Abel/Toronto Sun)

SUNshine Girl June   SUNshine Girl June is a month late, but we’re not complaining! The vibrant Virgo likes Dodge Chargers, country music, hip hop, modelling, working out and volleyball, while her dream job is to be a runway model. (Toronto Sun/Postmedia Network)

SUNshine Girl Alliyah   SUNshine Girl Alliyah is a Leo who rarely stops moving. She likes swimming, dancing, track, hoops and volleyball. She slows down to watch movies, sing and check social media. (Toronto Sun)
Jury finds Tesla one per cent negligent in fatal Model S crash   Autopilot was not at issue in this case

Why is stress so bad for our immune systems?   Study finds that stress not only impacts the heart and blood pressure, but it also ages our immune systems and makes it more difficult to fight infections, like COVID-19.

Hot dogs: Leaving car windows open is not enough to keep your pet safe from the heat   The temperature in a parked car, even in the shade with the windows partly open, can quickly heat up enough to seriously harm or even kill your pet.

COVID-19 daily update: Omicron boosters coming soon, tourists stranded in beach city and Saskatchewan Roughriders forced to sit out   From shutting down sports to ruining vacations, COVID-19 continues to spoil the fun as we churn through a seventh wave.

TikTok Tuesday: Parents of young girls who died trying the 'blackout challenge' suing TikTok   The lawsuit claims TikTok knew of the harmful effects of the 'blackout challenge,' but says the company did not effectively correct the algorithm or warn parents.

Pete Davidson says Louis C.K. tried to get him fired from Saturday Night Live due to his cannabis use   "Pete smokes so much weed that it makes people uncomfortable," the comedian reportedly told SNL's creator.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
Cannabis site worth $100K busted next to police station   Grower reportedly fainted when police made the journey across the street to inspect the operation.

American Israeli woman formerly jailed in Russia over cannabis talks about Brittney Griner's case   Naama Issachar had about nine grams of cannabis in her possession and recalls feeling her heart sink when a Russian airport official discovered a small plastic bag tucked inside a fanny pack.
OPP stop vehicle for stunt driving, charge two with list of drug-related offences   Two 20-year-old men face a combined 11 charges.
